body: PT Intikeramik Alamasri Industri Tbk (IKAI) is allocating IDR 40 billion in capital expenditure for 2025, with approximately 87% to be used for manufacturing purposes, including establishing a new production line. The company expects this investment to drive production growth of around 1 million square meters by 2026, with IDR 35 billion allocated for new machinery.
title: PT Intikeramik Alamasri Industri to Allocate IDR 40 Billion for Capital Expenditure in 2025

body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) closed 0.62% higher at 7,079.90, extending its uptrend from last week, with a transaction volume of 25.7 million shares and value of IDR 12.39 trillion, surpassing last week's daily averages. PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) led the top gainers with an 11.11% surge, contributing 15.76 points to the JCI, while PT Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI) was among the top laggards, recording a 1.72% decline.
title: Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index Closes 0.62% Higher at 7,079.90 Points

body: Foreign investors ended 2024 with a net sell of 384.96 million shares, with PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) topping the list with a net sell of 614.84 million shares, followed by PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) with a net sell of 57.60 million shares. However, PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BRMS) saw the largest net buy of 192.30 million shares, while other companies such as PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (BUKA) and PT BFI Finance Indonesia Tbk (BFI) also recorded significant net buys.
title: Foreign investors end 2024 with net buy position in Indonesian market despite selling shares in major companies.

body: PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BRMS) led the net foreign leaderboard with 96.24 million shares, with a transaction volume of 577.81 million shares worth IDR 194.06 billion, and its share price closed at IDR 346, a positive shift from IDR 332 last Friday. PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (BUKA) ranked second, with a net foreign of 68.13 million shares, and its share price closed at IDR 125, a slight increase from IDR 123 last Friday, with a transaction volume of 170.39 million shares worth IDR 21.12 billion.
title: PT Bumi Resources Minerals leads net foreign buying with 96.24 million shares on Monday's transaction.

body: PT Indonesia Paradise Property Tbk (INPP) is issuing a bond worth IDR 500 billion, consisting of two series: Series A with a 7.05% coupon rate and 3-year tenure, and Series B with a 7.30% coupon rate and 5-year tenure. The bond will be offered to the public from December 30, 2024, to January 3, 2025, and is expected to be listed on the Indonesia Stock Exchange on January 9, 2025, with joint underwriters including PT CIMB Niaga Sekuritas, PT Mandiri Sekuritas, and PT Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia Tbk.
title: Indonesia Paradise Property to Issue IDR 500 Billion Bond in Two Series with 3- and 5-Year Tenures.

body: PT Communication Cable Systems Indonesia Tbk (CCSI) is conducting a rights issue to raise IDR 51 billion by issuing 133.33 million new shares at IDR 383 per share, with the listing date set for January 10, 2025. The company's non-public shareholders, including PT Grahatama Kreasibaru, PT Saptadaya Bumitama Persada, PT Lingkar Matra, and Peter Djatmiko, will not participate in the rights issue, allowing Central Business Alliance Pte Ltd to claim a 10% stake in CCSI post-rights issue.
title: PT Communication Cable Systems Indonesia to Raise IDR 51 Billion through Rights Issue of 133.33 Million New Shares

body: PT Baramulti Suksessarana Tbk (BSSR) will distribute an interim dividend of USD 25 million, or IDR 154.39 per share, with a relatively modest dividend payout ratio of 8.4%, drawn from its net profit of USD 298.6 million as of September 2024. The interim dividend yield stands at approximately 3.5%, with shareholders set to receive the dividend on January 15, 2025, following cum-ex dividend dates on January 9-14, 2025.
title: PT Baramulti Suksesarana to Distribute USD 25 Million Interim Dividend for 2024 Fiscal Year

body: Jakarta's big market capitalization banks, including PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA), PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI), PT Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI), and PT Bank Negara Indonesia Tbk (BBNI), collectively slipped in the morning session, with BBCA down 1% to IDR 9,700 per share, BBRI down 0.24% to IDR 4,090, BMRI down 1.72% to IDR 5,700, and BBNI down 0.46% to IDR 4,340. The downtrend affected the Jakarta Composite Index, which decreased by 7,021.96 points to 7,026.13, as well as other indices comprising blue-chip stocks, such as LQ45, IDX30, and IDX80.
title: Jakarta's big market capitalisation banks experience collective slippage in morning session, affecting Jakarta Composite Index.

body: Barito Pacific (BRPT) has secured a credit facility of IDR 700 billion from Bank BTN to support its working capital requirements, which is expected to maintain its financial flexibility and boost operational activities. Despite a 25.22% year-on-year decline in net profit to USD 26.80 million in Q3 2024, the company's share price has risen 12.35% to IDR 910 per share as of December 27, 2024.
title: Barito Pacific Secures IDR 700 Billion Loan Facility to Support Working Capital Requirements.

body: PT Harum Energy Tbk (HRUM) has successfully swapped debt for equity in two of its subsidiaries, PT Tanito Harum Nickel and PT Harum Nickel Perkasa, with a total value of USD 411.91 million, resulting in HRUM owning 51% of PT Tanito Harum Nickel and 64.75% of PT Harum Nickel Perkasa. This debt-to-equity swap is part of a strategic partnership to consolidate ownership and management of nickel business assets, with PT Puring Investasi Indonesia and PT Aster Investasi Indonesia holding the remaining stakes.
title: PT Harum Energy completes debt-to-equity swap for two nickel subsidiaries, gaining optimism for the industry.
